name: Ohio
description: The Buckeye State — its presidents, cities, sports, and quirks.

questions.q: What is the capital of Ohio?
What is Ohio's official nickname?

source.notes: Named after the abundance of Ohio buckeye trees (Aesculus glabra).

questions.q: In what year was Ohio admitted as the 17th U.S. state?

source.notes: First state from the Northwest Territory.

questions.q: Which famous brothers — born/raised in Dayton, Ohio — invented the first powered airplane?

source.notes: Wilbur and Orville Wright, owners of a Dayton bicycle shop.

questions.q: Which Ohio city hosts the Pro Football Hall of Fame?

source.notes: Located in Canton because the NFL was founded there in 1920.

questions.q: Which Ohio city is known as the 'Rubber Capital of the World'?

source.notes: Home to Goodyear, Firestone, and other tire giants.

questions.q: Which Ohio city, sitting on Lake Erie, hosts the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame?

source.notes: Designed by I.M. Pei; opened in 1995.

questions.q: What is unique about Ohio's state flag, adopted in 1902?
choices: Is the only non-rectangular U.S. state flag (swallowtail/burgee)

questions.q: Approximately how many U.S. presidents were born in Ohio (often called 'Mother of Presidents')?

source.notes: Grant, Hayes, Garfield, B. Harrison, McKinley, Taft, Harding (8 if you count W. Harrison who lived in OH).

questions.q: Which U.S. president — Ohio's most prominent Civil War general — was born in Point Pleasant, Ohio?
Which Ohio-born inventor created the phonograph and the practical incandescent light bulb?
Ohio's three biggest cities all start with the same letter. Which one?
Which Ohio city, on the Ohio River, is home to MLB's Reds and the NFL's Bengals?
Which prestigious public university in Columbus is best known for its football program?

source.notes: The Buckeyes have won 8 college football national championships.

questions.q: Which NBA superstar was born in Akron, Ohio in 1984?
Which famous Ohio-born astronaut was the first American to orbit Earth (1962)?

source.notes: Born in Cambridge, Ohio. Later U.S. Senator. (Neil Armstrong was also Ohio-born — Wapakoneta.)

questions.q: Which Ohio-born NASA astronaut was the first person to walk on the Moon (1969)?
Which 1995 baseball film starring Charlie Sheen was set with a fictional Ohio team called the Indians?

source.notes: Sequel to 'Major League' (1989); set in Cleveland.

questions.q: Which famous Ohio-born horror author wrote the Goosebumps series?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced contradictory book list (The Stand/Shining/It are Stephen King's, who is from Maine). R.L. Stine of 'Goosebumps' was born in Columbus, Ohio.

questions.q: Which lake forms Ohio's northern boundary?

source.notes: Cleveland and Toledo sit on its shore.
